meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve can't scale projects (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't scale projects. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve Scaling Issues
content.seo_description: Stop guessing why your apps fail to scale. Learn how to implement professional analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code to drive data-informed growth.
content.answer_block: Scaling issues often stem from a lack of visibility. By integrating event-based analytics early, you transform guesswork into actionable data. Use Antigravity to instrument your core user flows and Claude Code to automate the integration, allowing you to identify bottlenecks, optimize conversion paths, and build features users actually need.
content.intro:
You’ve deployed your first project, but the momentum flatlines. You’re staring at a dashboard of zero, wondering if users are bouncing or if the app is simply invisible. This isn't a failure of your code—it’s a failure of your feedback loop. Without data, you are flying blind, unable to distinguish between a technical bug and a design friction point.

Today, we shift from 'coding in the dark' to 'engineering with precision.' By implementing a robust analytics layer, you gain the superpower of knowing exactly where your users drop off. This tutorial bridges the gap between a hobbyist project and a scalable SaaS, using the latest AI-native tools to automate the heavy lifting of instrumentation.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the three key events that define user success. This prevents 'data noise' and keeps your tracking lean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest the 3 most critical user interaction events to track for a SaaS application to measure user retention and feature adoption.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze my current project structure and identify the top 3 user events that indicate successful onboarding.'

steps.title: Initialize the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Standardize your tracking library. We'll set up a centralized service to ensure events are sent asynchronously without blocking the main thread.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular analytics service class that wraps standard tracking calls, ensuring error handling and asynchronous execution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a new analytics service file in /services/analytics.ts and export a trackEvent function.'

steps.title: Instrument Critical User Flows
steps.description: Inject tracking into high-intent actions like sign-ups, feature toggles, and payment completions to map the user journey.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scan my login and signup components and inject the trackEvent function into the submit handlers, capturing relevant user metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Inject trackEvent calls into all form submission handlers in the /components directory.'

steps.title: Implement Session Replay for Debugging
steps.description: Beyond events, you need context. Session replays show you exactly what the user saw during a crash or a drop-off.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Guide me through adding session replay tracking to my React app, ensuring I follow privacy best practices by masking sensitive input fields.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add session replay integration to the main App component with input masking enabled.'

steps.title: Verify Data Flow and Testing
steps.description: Ensure your events are reaching the server correctly. We use automated tests to verify the payload structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of integration tests that trigger user actions and verify that the correct analytics events are dispatched to the collector.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Verify that clicking the sign-up button triggers the correct analytics event with expected payload.'

pro_tips: Always track the 'exit intent' event; it is the most valuable data point for understanding why users leave your platform.
Never send PII (Personally Identifiable Information) to analytics services; use anonymized UUIDs for user identification.
Batch your tracking events on the client side to reduce network overhead and improve your Core Web Vitals.
Use custom dimensions to track which version or A/B test variant the user is currently experiencing.
Set up real-time alerts for 'error' events to fix bugs before your users even realize they exist.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, wait for 100 sessions, then analyze the drop-off points. Use Claude Code to refactor the specific components where users are abandoning the flow.
content.conversion_block: You now have the visibility to turn your project into a growth engine. Stop guessing and start scaling with precision. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
